key_observations: Severe volume collapse: 73% drop from Week 1 (23.6km) to Week 5 (6.3km), suggesting either injury, illness, or burnout requiring immediate investigation
5-week gap (May 13 to May 29) with minimal activity indicates significant training disruption; recovery incomplete given modest Week 6 restart
Pace maintained consistently (5:39–6:18/km) despite reduced volume suggests aerobic base preserved, but HR data absence (5 of 6 weeks) prevents fatigue assessment
warnings: Critical undertraining: averaging only 1.3 runs/week over 8 weeks with inconsistent scheduling—pattern inconsistent with serious athletic development
Data deficiency: HR unavailable for 83% of training data—cannot assess intensity distribution, overtraining risk, or recovery quality
Detraining risk: 5-week layoff in May represents significant aerobic loss; Week 6 return at 15.7km is appropriate but requires 2-3 weeks consolidation before progression
recommendations: Clarify training intent: determine whether this is intentional tapering for a June event, injury-induced rest, or lifestyle constraint—adjust programming accordingly
Enable HR/intensity tracking: configure device to capture HR on all runs to monitor aerobic capacity, zone distribution, and recovery markers going forward
Rebuild systematically: establish 4-week base phase at 3 runs/week (15–20km total) at conversational pace before advancing intensity; current 1-run/week frequency insufficient for adaptation

weeks_analysis.assessment: Strong opening: 3 runs, solid 23.6km volume, consistent 6:06–6:18 pace. Good aerobic foundation established.
Sharp 42% drop to single 13.67km run. May indicate planned recovery week or unplanned disruption—context unknown.
Continued decline: 30% reduction, single 9.5km run after 12-day gap. Pattern suggests illness, injury, or external life stress.
Bottoming out: lowest volume (7.2km), single easy-effort run. Potential detraining phase if unintentional.
Nadir: minimal 6.3km, single run. Zero training stimulus—critical intervention point if training goals exist.
Partial recovery: 150% spike to 15.7km, 3 runs including football cross-training (HR 158 avg). Appropriate restart pace; HR data finally available.
